content: The Government has accepted the recommendations of the 7th CPC and implemented them vide Notification dated 11.12.2018. Clarifications regarding amendments in CCS (Leave) Rules, 1972 are issued.
The amendments made in the CCS (Leave) Rules, 1972 vide Notification dated 11.12.2018 came into force with effect from 14.12.2018.
Government servants serving in a Vacation Department have been allowed Earned Leave in place of Half Pay Leave by amending Rule 28 and Rule 29.
CCL may be granted at 100% leave salary for the first 365 days and 80% leave salary for the next 365 days.
CCL may be extended to single male parents including unmarried, widower, and divorcee employees.
Single female Government servants may avail CCL in six spells per calendar year. Other eligible Government servants may avail a maximum of three spells per calendar year.

section: Work Related Illness and Injury Leave
content: Special Disability Leave has been replaced by Work Related Illness and Injury Leave (WRIIL) for illness or injury attributable to official duties.

section: Work Related Illness and Injury Leave
content: Hospital Leave and Special Disability Leave for accidental injury have been deleted and replaced by WRIIL.

section: Work Related Illness and Injury Leave
content: Employees on WRIIL are entitled to full pay and allowances during the entire period of hospitalization.
Government servants other than military officers receive full pay and allowances for six months after hospitalization and Half Pay for twelve months thereafter.
CAPF officers receive full pay and allowances for six months after hospitalization and full pay for the next twenty-four months.
Personnel below officer rank in CAPF receive full pay and allowances without any limit on the leave period.
No Earned Leave or Half Pay Leave is credited during the period an employee is on WRIIL.
